But first thing, so what is ICP?
ICP stands for ideal customer profile, if you're not familiar.
And it's something that it's pretty standard with like product teams trying to figure out who is the person that they need to serve and if the product serves them enough.
And when it does, it's the important part of product creation that we usually call product market fit.

And if you find the two things combined, and then you have a good chance of being able to scale and have like a steady, more of a steady growth until somebody comes back, comes in and disrupt you again.
But like, that's usually what you're shooting for in the beginning.
And it's very hard to find a small enough group that you can build exactly things for them, but it's large enough to make a good business.
So it's always an exercise to be constantly changing things and reprioritizing.

But the way to think about it's like, first, you think about the whole market, like who is, who is the target audience and what are their pain points in our case, like, it's very easy to like every, every company in the world will probably need our services, like you need like, marketing work, if you just narrow it down to content creation, you need a website, and you need content.
That website.
That's too broad.
And even if you focus more on like, okay, only the companies that are business to business and they need high volume content, that's still too broad.
So you can't build any product, you can't build any processes around that.
So you go down one more level and then you can start thinking about, okay, what are the pain points and do they care about the stuff that we do enough?
And then you can start thinking about, maybe they have like prioritization around their markets.
Are they in a super competitive market or not?
Do they really value the content creation as a key part of marketing strategy compared to just ads or doing something else?
And if you go down, a next level would be like, can we actually serve them?
Can we do that without overstretching the team or can we do that with the features we have?
So like, because our business has a two-sided component where like our team is doing the work.
And we're trying to also build the product to support the team.
We have to marry both things.
If we promise too much and we overstretch the team, that's not good.
And if we promise too much and we don't have enough features to support the team, that's not good either.
So what is something that there is enough pain points, meaning the target audience is there, there is a fit as they care about our stuff, and we are ready to serve them.
And so those are the three main things that we're looking for.
And the readiness part is the part that now that we have enough engineers and designers in the team, we can start increasing our readiness, but we're not quite there with certain things yet.
So that's the projects that I'll walk you through at the end.
But the way we're thinking about is essentially, oh, sorry, the slide jumped.
It's around four main areas.

Financial health, are they stable enough as a company?

Or are they running out of money and all that stuff?
Content foundation, do they know how hard content creation is?
Like if we had Marcel as a client at DeepGram, he would be great because he knows how hard content creation is.
And if you're trying to do content that's scary, how even harder that gets.
But not everybody's like Marcel.
So like if we can find Marcel times 100, that would be the perfect setup.
And another third thing is like strategic alignment.
Like we're trying to do things in a different way.
So like a lot of things we're trying to automate, we're trying to make it faster to create content, high quality content in volume.
So like if we have a client that's like one example, if they're stuck on like they really need to generate images in this way.
And that's just not the way you can generate today with models.
Even if we squeeze those models as much as possible.
So that's going to be a bottleneck in the process that they care about that, you know?
So those are some of the four, those are the four areas we were thinking about.
And like I added all the different, like a little bit of an example for each, like the items that we triage.
